title: scurrula pentandra
intro: Scurrula pentandra, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species native to Asia and Australia, belonging to the family Santalaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Scurrula pentandra include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. Scurrula pentandra can be prepared as an herbal medicine through methods such as decoction, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various applications and dosages depending on the intended use.
